Metadata.siteDescription: Yhoset Gonzalez's (YHOSINC) portfolio: interfaces built with ink & code. Projects, skills, a learning roadmap, and mini-tools for devs and designers.

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: Case studies and shipped projects by YHOSINC — frontend, product design, and AI, with the technical detail behind each delivery.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: YHOSINC's stack and disciplines: frontend, design systems, motion, 3D/WebGL, backend, research, prototyping, and shipping — filterable by area.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.description: YHOSINC's learning path on an interactive canvas: junior to senior, across programming, design, and artificial intelligence.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Free mini-tools for people on the same path: palette extractor, gradient generator, type scale, comic shadow, easing editor, and Manga-fy.

about.title: About
about.description: Who Yhoset Gonzalez (YHOSINC) is: his path blending graphic design and programming, learning fast and wanting to do things right.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Write to YHOSINC — design and code. A direct contact form, no middlemen.

account.title: Account
account.description: Create a visitor account to comment on YHOSINC's projects.

Footer.tagline: Ink & code — programming, design, and AI.

HomePage.body: I design systems that feel like the splash page of a manga — loud where it matters, precise where it counts. Learning fast, and wanting to leave everything properly done.

Projects.title: Projects
Projects.emptyBody: Case studies are coming soon — in the meantime, you can check the project roadmap at internal/v2-next/arquitectura.md.
Projects.emptyFilteredBody: No published projects use this skill yet.

Roadmap.emptyTitle: The canvas has no milestones yet
Roadmap.emptyBody: The learning path (junior → mid → senior, across programming, design, and AI) is coming soon.

About.title: About
About.paragraph1: I got into programming almost by accident: friends kept telling me "this might interest you," and they were right. That's where I really discovered what programming is, and I got hooked — it's a field I love, especially because I can mix it with something I already loved: graphic design. I can design pretty much anything, and code lets me carry those ideas past the page.
About.paragraph2: I'm a versatile person — I like getting my hands into all kinds of tasks, and above all, leaving things properly done, not half-finished.
About.paragraph3: I'm still building up real experience, but I'm learning fast — every new project is an excuse to go deeper.

Tools.title: Tools
Tools.body: Small utilities for people on the same path — code and design.

paleta.title: Palette extractor
paleta.description: Upload an image and pull out its dominant colors as hex, ready to copy.

gradientes.title: Gradient generator
gradientes.description: Build a linear or radial CSS gradient with multiple color stops and copy the declaration.

escala-tipografica.title: Type scale
escala-tipografica.description: Generate a full modular scale (musical ratios) with a real preview and CSS variables.

sombra-comic.title: Comic shadow
sombra-comic.description: This very site's hard-shadow technique, adjustable — for panels with a comic-book edge.

editor-de-easing.title: Easing editor
editor-de-easing.description: Build a cubic-bezier curve by dragging control points, with a real animated preview.

mangafy.title: Manga-fy
mangafy.description: Turn a photo into ink outlines + halftone screentone, manga-page style. A 100% in-browser image-processing filter — not an AI model.

paletteTool.dropzone: Drag an image here, or click to choose a file
paletteTool.error: Couldn't read that image. Try a different file.

mangafyTool.dropzone: Drag a photo here, or click to choose a file
mangafyTool.error: Couldn't read that image. Try a different file.
